response.justification: Candidate has strong full-stack credentials and 15+ years of experience, but the role centers on hands-on C++ development for industrial automation and the resume shows only a brief 6-month C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014–2015 plus an academic bachelor thesis. The recent decade has been TypeScript/Node/DLang focused, with no exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, IEC 61850), or message brokers in an industrial context. The profile better fits a senior web/full-stack role than a C++ platform engineer position.
strengths: 15+ years of software experience with full-stack breadth and strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)
Background in low-latency, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, microservices)
Demonstrated mentorship and teaching ability via workshops, dojos, and Selenium courses
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, over a decade ago; no recent STL/OOP C++ work
No evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity expertise
No exposure to MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, or industrial automation domain

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / SDL background
Strong senior-level engineering maturity and mentorship
